Try to more simply describe semi-auto.

This might be the issue: 

- Your camera config file references `majorStream`, which is generated from `--create`.  
- My own camera config files reference `mainStream`.  

This appears to be bug on my account (or the names changed at some point - which seems unlikely).  What streams are available are reported, and your `user_output.txt` does report `mainStream` as one of them.

If this is the problem, I need to correct the text generated by `--create` and come up with a better error message than `Camera doesn't appear to be active, will wait.` should be something like `Could not connect to stream XYZ. Is this the correct stream name? Is the camera active? Waiting in case it becomes available.`

Being unfamiliar with the async and all this camera stuff, the vague message is probably the result of a vague understanding when I wrote it.
Should be fixed.  I also did some code and messaging cleanups.  Everything seems to still work for my two cameras.
It looks like I forgot to add the code to get the value from the config. It shouldn't take long to fix.  Probably tomorrow.

If/when I get around to thinking about cloud options, I'll revisit you note.  Some years ago I had a pi-cam setup running motioneye that uploaded to google drive - I can't recall how that was achieved, but that might be an option too.

When I first got it, my C225 was not reporting `isPeople`, I asked about this in the tp-link forums and got a [reply](https://community.tp-link.com/en/smart-home/forum/topic/737798?replyId=1476656).  The resultant firmware update solved the issue.  They responded very quickly - a couple of days as I recall.  Earlier posts in the thread detail the logs I posted that got their attention.

I later purchased a C125, `isPeople` worked out of the box.

It might be worth checking for new firmware or raising a question with tp-link.

Yes, I wanted to use a raspberry pi and not consume too much CPU and power, so I decided to try and leverage the camera's AI.  It's mostly worked well, except for the one time I had to reboot the cameras (maybe I did something to upset them - messing with the Wifi or wired-network).

I note that tp-link will shortly introduce the [Tapo H500](https://www.tp-link.com/us/home-networking/smart-hub/tapo-h500/) _home base_ to my local market. That sounds like it might be hassle free way to go for local monitoring and recording.  I may well move in that direction.

Check that all items match when assessing presets bound to points in lux-profiles; make sure to only check available VDUs. Eliminate doubling up of points when loading lux-profiles with bound Presets; fixes incorrect rejection of Presets when assessing brightness/lux.

Revert "Tesing a tag change"
